facts: A plant is a living thing because it grows and needs water, even though it does not move from place to place like an animal.
An animal is a living thing because it can move on its own and needs food and water, while a toy only moves when someone pushes it.
Living things respond to their surroundings — for example, a flower turns toward the sun, but a plastic flower never changes direction.
misconceptions: A plant is not alive because it does not move.
